input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>Large, leafless trees with rough, gray bark dominate the foreground, casting dark shadows on their trunks. A clear blue sky is visible through the branches. The ground is blanketed in white snow, with accumulated snowdrifts at the base of the trees and extending into the background woods. The scene remains static with no observable movement or changes in lighting.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

A brown log cabin is visible behind the left tree, while the dark shelter remains in the background right, completing the winter scene under the bright sky.

The video opens with a stationary view of two large tree trunks with rough bark in the foreground, set against a brown log cabin with snow-covered roofs and a clear blue sky. The camera tilts upward to reveal bare branches extending into the sky, momentarily shifting the cabin to the lower frame, before tilting back down to focus on the trunks' mid-sections. The left trunk is distinguished by a vertical series of dark hollows. As the shot holds steady, the brown cabin with white-framed windows remains visible in the background left, surrounded by snow drifts on the ground and deck. Finally, the camera pans slightly left, centering the hollowed trunk while the adjacent trunk shifts toward the right edge, maintaining the bright winter setting.

[116-118] The camera tilts slightly upward, bringing the snow-covered green plastic object and the rough tree trunk behind it into clearer view on the left. Light-colored wood chips remain scattered on the white snow surface between the green item and the wooden structure positioned on the right side.

The view ascends to reveal vertical hollows and textured gray bark against a backdrop of bare trees and a clear blue sky. A dark structure is visible in the distance.

[120-122] The camera tilts further upward along the rough, gray tree trunk, passing the vertical hollow to show upper branches against a clear blue sky. The snow-covered ground and green plastic object are no longer visible. A dark structure stands in the distant background among bare trees. The bark texture remains prominent as the view ascends.

queries.question: How many tree trunks are visible in the foreground cluster?

samples.output: <think>The camera tilts upward, moving past the mid-section of the rough, gray tree trunk to focus on the upper branches against a clear blue sky. The textured bark remains central as bare limbs extend outward, completely excluding the snowy ground and lower trunk from the frame. The bright sky becomes the primary background.</think><answer></answer>
